title: Master AI in Just 10 Hours: Google Launches Game-Changing AI Professional Certificate on Coursera
summary: Google has officially launched its first AI Professional Certificate, expanding its Generative AI catalog on Coursera, which has already attracted nearly three million enrollments globally. This new program is designed to help professionals integrate artificial intelligence into their daily work with practical, job-ready skills that go beyond basic prompting. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); As AI fluency rapidly becomes a baseline expectation across industries, this certificate positions learners to stay competitive in an evolving workforce. According to recent research conducted by Google and IPSOS, more than half of job postings requesting AI skills are now outside traditional IT and computer science roles. However, only 40% of American workers currently use AI at work. This certificate aims to close that gap. What Makes the Google AI Professional Certificate Unique? The Google AI Professional Certificate focuses on universal, transferable skills developed through analysis of hundreds of job descriptions and collaboration with employers. It emphasizes hands-on learning and responsible AI use. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Every learner who enrolls receives: Three months of no-cost access to Google AI Pro Practical experience using Google s advanced AI models A portfolio of real-world AI projects Additionally, Google is offering free access to the certificate for U.S. small businesses, alongside tools such as Google Workspace Business Standard to support AI adoption nationwide. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Program Structure and Courses The certificate can be completed in approximately 10 hours and includes seven short courses plus a hands-on capstone project. Courses include: AI Fundamentals Learn foundational generative AI concepts and responsible evaluation of AI outputs. AI for Brainstorming and Planning Use AI to generate structured project plans. AI for Research and Insights Synthesize information for faster decision-making. AI for Writing and Communication Craft persuasive and audience-specific messaging. AI for Content Creation Develop and critique multimedia assets responsibly. AI for Data Analysis Convert complex datasets into clear, actionable insights. AI for App Building Build a custom AI-powered solution using natural language. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Throughout the program, learners work with tools such as Gemini, NotebookLM, and AI Studio, gaining hands-on experience directly from Google experts. Skills You Will Gain By the end of the certificate, participants will have developed: Reusable AI prompts Data-driven project plans AI-generated research and visual content A custom AI-powered application Strong understanding of responsible AI practices ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); The program is ideal for working professionals seeking to future-proof their careers and organizations aiming to upskill their teams. Deadline No official deadline has been announced. Enrollment is currently open. APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity , click here. For more opportunities, click here. title: Youth, Peace and Security Online Course 2026: UN-Certified Training to Implement the YPS Agenda
summary: Build Practical Skills to Advance Youth Participation in Peace and Security Young people are increasingly recognized as powerful agents of change in preventing conflict, sustaining peace, and shaping inclusive security policies. To translate global commitments into real-world impact, practitioners need the right skills, frameworks, and tools. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); Realizing the Youth, Peace and Security Agenda is a comprehensive, practitioner-focused online course jointly developed by United Nations System Staff College and the Folke Bernadotte Academy . The programme equips professionals working in peace and security with the knowledge and applied competencies needed to effectively implement the Youth, Peace and Security (YPS) agenda while working with and for young people. Why This Course Matters The adoption of landmark UN Security Council resolutions 2250 (2015), 2419 (2018), and 2535 (2020) elevated youth participation in peace and security from aspiration to international obligation. Despite progress, implementation remains uneven across local, national, and global levels. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); This course responds directly to that gap by offering a structured, skills-based learning journey that strengthens institutional capacity and supports young women and men as contributors to peaceful and just societies. Course Overview Language: English Format: Fully online Duration: Approximately 2 months of active learning (spread across 2026) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Weekly workload: 3 4 hours Course fee: USD 1,250 Enrollment Deadline: 10 April 2026 APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . The programme blends self-paced modules, live expert-led webinars, peer exchange, and written assignments. Participants enjoy flexible access via the UNSSC online learning platform, enabling learning from anywhere in the world. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Course Structure and Timeline Part I YPS Foundational Skills 13 April 22 May 2026 Module 1: Introduction to the Youth, Peace and Security Agenda Module 2: Enabling meaningful youth participation Module 3: Conducting youth-sensitive conflict analysis ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Includes one mandatory written assignment. Part II YPS Applied Skills (Elective Modules) 14 September 30 October 2026 Module 4: Serving as a YPS adviser or focal point Module 5: Youth-responsive and YPS-focused programming Module 6: In-country implementation of the YPS Agenda ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Includes at least one written assignment. Successful completion requires finishing all foundational modules and at least one elective module with assignments. Learning Methodology Participants engage in: Guided self-study modules Live interactive webinars (six in total) Peer learning and experience sharing Individual feedback and one-to-one facilitator support This approach ensures both conceptual understanding and practical application of YPS principles. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Who Should Apply The course is designed for practitioners and representatives from: The UN system International and regional security organizations Non-governmental organizations Civil society organizations working on youth, peace, and security Selection Process Selection is competitive and based on: Quality of motivation statement Relevant experience and knowledge Commitment and availability Diversity across gender, geography, and sector Willingness to actively contribute to peer learning UN applicants must also submit a supervisor s endorsement as part of the online application. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Discover more learning opportunities here . title: Transform Your Institution with the FATE Foundation AI Upskilling Programme 2026 — Empower Students and Lecturers for the AI Revolution
summary: https://wp.me/p23f03-jtXAs Artificial Intelligence reshapes global industries, African universities must lead, not follow. The FATE Foundation AI Upskilling Programme is a high-impact, continent-wide initiative designed to equip accredited Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) with world-class AI teaching capacity, research frameworks, and student readiness skills all at no cost to your institution. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); What Is the FATE Foundation AI Upskilling Programme? The AI Upskilling Programme is a multi-country initiative led by the FATE Foundation in partnership with the African Institute for Mathematical Sciences (AIMS) and supported by Google.org with a strategic $4 million investment to build a sustainable AI education ecosystem across Africa. The programme empowers institutions in: Kenya Nigeria Ghana South Africa to integrate advanced AI competencies into teaching, research, and curriculum design enabling them to produce graduates ready for tomorrow s AI-powered economy . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Who Should Apply? (Eligibility Criteria) To qualify for the 2026 intake, institutions must: 1. Be an accredited Higher Education Institution (HEI) 2. Offer STEM-related programmes (science, tech, engineering, math) 3. Demonstrate technical infrastructure readiness 4. Commit to integrating AI modules into curricula 5. Support participation from lecturers, teaching assistants, and senior STEM students 6. Participate in monitoring evaluation and capstone projects Note: Individual lecturers and students apply through their nominated institution there is no direct individual application. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Benefits for Institutions Participants 1.World-Class AI Training Participating institutions receive: Free AI training for at least four lecturers per institution. Certification for trained faculty. Localized curriculum resources for institutional use. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); 2.Curriculum Teaching Resources Curriculum modules are based on the Google DeepMind AI Research Foundation framework , covering: Small language model creation Neural network design and training Transformer architectures Model fine-tuning and alignment Real-world capstone projects This curriculum is adapted with local context to ensure relevance in African higher education systems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); 3.Student Empowerment Students benefit from: Advanced AI competencies aligned with global industry needs. Opportunities to build AI solutions for real-world problems. Mentorship and exposure to applied AI research. 4.Institutional Recognition Institutions gain: Visibility as innovators in AI education. Potential networking with African institutions and ecosystem partners. Contribution to Africa s developing AI ecosystem. Who Can Participate Internally? Lecturers Become certified AI Champions capable of leading instruction. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Teaching Assistants (TAs) Support AI learning and curriculum delivery. Penultimate Final-Year STEM Students Gain cutting-edge, industry-relevant AI skills. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); How to Apply Step by Step Apply here Complete the online registration form with accurate institutional and contact details. Submit before March 31 2026 (recommended). Await confirmation and further onboarding instructions. Prepare lecturers and materials for training and curriculum integration. Review eligibility guidelines and participation commitments. For questions or technical support, check the contact information on the official page or email the programme team as listed. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Key Deadlines Application Timeline Milestone Date / Status Applications Open Now Ongoing Suggested Submission Deadline 31st March 2026 (for priority review) Ongoing Review Selection Rolling basis Notifications to Selected Institutions Following review cycle Programme Implementation Window 2026 academic year Applications are reviewed periodically, and early submissions are highly recommended to secure institutional onboarding. AI skills are rapidly becoming critical in education, research, and industry. Programs like this enable African universities to lead technological innovation rather than be late adopters . This initiative is strategically positioned to: Build local expertise in AI research and teaching competencies. Strengthen technology ecosystems across multiple African nations. Advance employability and innovation capacity for students.
